latest.title: Repayment of c.$48 million of Senior Notes
latest.content:
Today 09:32
RNS Number : 5622J
Tullow Oil PLC
24 June 2026
Tullow Oil plc
Repayment of c.$48 million of Senior Notes
24 June 2026
-
Tullow Oil plc (Tullow)
announces a c.$48 million repayment of its outstanding Senior Secured Notes due 2028 (the "Notes").
Richard Miller, Chief Financial Officer of Tullow, commented:
"This partial repayment of the Notes has been enabled by our strong operational and financial performance in 2026. Being able to take this meaningful step only two months after closing the refinancing transaction is reflective of our disciplined approach to capital allocation and the quality of our assets. We continue to focus on deleveraging and exploring longer term refinancing and strategic options, underpinned by our enhanced value proposition."
Key redemption details
·
Issuer:
Tullow Holdco 2 Limited
·
Notes:
10.25% Senior Secured Notes due 2028
·
ISIN:
USG9131AAA19 (Reg S) and US89941AAA07 (Rule 144A)
CUSIP:
G9131A AA1 (Reg S) and 89941A AA0 (Rule 144A)
·
Redemption date
:
24 June 2026
·
Principal amount repaid (Redeemed Notes):
$48,211,589.95
·
Redemption Price:
101% of the principal amount
·
Interest
: the following cash payments in respect of interest accrued between 24 April 2026 (the "Closing Date") and the Redemption Date have been made on the Redemption Date:
o
uncapitalised Mandatory PIK Interest on the Redeemed Notes;
o
Mandatory Cash Interest on the Redeemed Notes;
o
uncapitalised PIYC Interest on the outstanding principal amount of the Notes prior to the Redemption Date.
·
Remaining outstanding principal post repayment:
$1,162,034,475
Capitalized terms used but not defined herein have the meanings ascribed to such terms in the Indenture.
Execution mechanism
The redemption is executed in accordance with the provisions outlined in the indenture dated as of 17 May 2021 and as amended and restated on 10 April 2026 (as amended, restated, supplemented or otherwise modified from time to time, the "Indenture").
In accordance with the Indenture the redemption will take place on the basis of a pro rata pass-through distribution of principal. No Notes of $1,000 or less will be redeemed in part.
Partial redemption including reduction of Global Notes and issuance of replacement Definitive Registered Notes if applicable will be effected in accordance with DTC procedures.
CONTACTS
Tullow Investor Relations
ir@tullowoil.com
Matthew Evans
Julia Bullock (Corporate Finance Manager)
Camarco (Media)
(+44 20 3757 4980)
Billy Clegg
Georgia Edmonds
Rebecca Waterworth
Paying Agent:
GLAS Trust Company LLC (739423)
3 Second Street, Suite 203
Jersey City, NJ 07311
United States of America
Att: Trustee and Escrow Services / Tullow Oil
tes@glas.agency
Notes to editors
Tullow is an independent energy company committed to building a better future through the responsible oil and gas development of its core producing assets in Ghana. The Group is quoted on the London and Ghanaian stock exchanges (symbol: TLW). For further information, please refer to:
www.tullowoil.com
.
Follow Tullow on:
LinkedIn:
www.linkedin.com/company/Tullow-Oil
X:
www.X.com/TullowOilplc

latest.title: Director/PDMR Shareholding
latest.content:
Today 15:46
RNS Number : 3647I
Tullow Oil PLC
15 June 2026
NOTIFICATION OF TRANSACTIONS OF PERSONS DISCHARGING MANAGERIAL RESPONSIBILITIES
Tullow announces that the following conditional and nil-cost option awards (each an "
Award
") over its ordinary shares of £0.10 pence each ("
Shares
") were made under the Tullow Oil plc 2023 Executive Share Plan ("
the ESP
") on 11 June 2026 to Ian Perks, Chief Executive Officer and Richard Miller, Chief Financial Officer of the Company, each being 'persons discharging managerial responsibilities'.
The vesting of the Awards depends upon the extent to which specified performance targets are met over a three-year performance period from 11 June 2026 to 10 June 2029, subject to any adjustments that are made to the vesting outcome of an Award in accordance with the rules of the ESP. Following any such vesting, the Awards will ordinarily be subject to a further two year "holding period" and the release of Shares to the relevant Award holder will be postponed until the expiry of that holding period (
i.e.
11 June 2031).
Separately, on 11 June 2026 Mr Perks and Mr Miller were each granted a cash conditional award (a "
Cash Award
") under the ESP (as amended and approved by shareholders by resolution at the annual general meeting of the Company held on 10 June 2026 (the "
2026 AGM
"). Each Cash Award represents a right for the relevant Cash Award holder to receive a cash sum in the maximum amount of 125% of their base salary (as at the date of grant of the Cash Award). The vesting of the Cash Awards (and the amount of cash payable to the relevant Cash Award holder) depends upon the extent to which specified performance targets (which relate to addressing the Company's debt maturities) are met over the vesting period of the Cash Award, subject to any adjustments that are made to the vesting outcome of an Award in accordance with the rules of the ESP. The Cash Awards have been granted in accordance with the terms of the new Directors' Remuneration Policy which was approved by resolution of the shareholders at the 2026 AGM. For the avoidance of doubt, the Cash Awards do not confer a right to receive shares, and the cash value that is receivable is not related to the price of a share - accordingly, details of the Cash Awards are not included in the regulatory tables set out in the remainder of this announcement.
Ian Perks

company_30s.what_they_do: TLW is a UK‑based company that builds and operates offshore wind farms. It develops projects from the early planning stage through construction to long‑term operations, selling the electricity it generates to national grid operators.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The offshore wind sector is a cornerstone of the UK’s net‑zero strategy. TLW’s projects tap into the growing demand for renewable energy, giving it exposure to a massive, policy‑driven market that is expected to expand rapidly over the next decade.
company_30s.current_state: The company is currently restructuring its balance sheet after a recent refinancing that cut a large chunk of senior debt. It has added new independent directors and applied for a block listing to improve share liquidity, signalling a focus on governance and financial stability. The stock is trading well below its historical valuation, indicating a steep discount to the company’s underlying assets.

rns_narrative: TLW has been busy shuffling its capital structure: a big debt repayment, a fresh refinancing that brought in new capital, and a block‑listing move to give investors a clearer exit route.
The board has been reshuffled with four new independent directors, which could bring fresh oversight and help rebuild investor confidence.
Shareholder activity is intense, with the largest block of shares being floated on the market to improve liquidity and reduce the concentration risk.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silence – the community isn’t talking about TLW right now.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: None – there are no bullish voices in the chatter, which is a classic contrarian green‑light.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: The few comments that exist focus on the company’s heavy debt load and the uncertainty around future project approvals.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Still muted – no change in sentiment, keeping the setup neutral for now.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – the topic hasn’t been searched, so a surprise catalyst could ignite a big move.

near_term_catalysts: A formal announcement of a new offshore wind contract or a power‑purchase agreement would validate the company’s project pipeline.
If TLW secures a new financing round or receives a regulatory approval for a key project, the market will see a clear turnaround signal.
A volume spike on the daily chart coupled with a bounce off the key support level could act as a technical confirmation.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden increase in trading volume, a positive earnings release, or a headline about a new partnership with a major energy player. Any of these would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: TLW sits at the tail end of its crash‑recovery cycle. The tight APEX compression suggests a short‑term squeeze is possible, but the real trigger will likely be a fundamental announcement rather than a purely technical bounce.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is down far more than 70 % from its peak, creating a huge upside if it recovers to halfway between the crash and the pre‑crash price. A deep crash often rewards the patient contrarian – the mean‑reversion can be explosive once a catalyst appears.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still carries a sizeable debt load and its future projects depend on regulatory approvals that may not materialise. If the refinancing fails or a key project is delayed, the price could stay depressed with limited upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside of roughly 200 % if the price climbs to the mid‑crash level, against a downside limited to the current price. The asymmetry is clear: the risk is capped at the current level, while the reward is several times higher.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price bounces off the last major support and volume confirms the move. Size a position that is 5–10 % of your portfolio, place a stop just below the support level, and target the halfway point between the crash low and the pre‑crash high.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small position until you see either a volume spike, a new contract announcement, or a regulatory green light. Only then move to a full trade.
playbook.if_passing: If the company is delisted, files for bankruptcy, or a major project is cancelled, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: TLW is a deep‑crashed, low‑liquidity play that could rebound sharply once a new offshore wind contract or financing announcement surfaces, offering a highly asymmetric upside with limited downside.
